flags.notes: THE EYES ARE THE STAR OF THIS PAGE. The spider's body is dark, but every eye must stay lively: light sclera, deep near-black pupil, bright white catchlight. Do NOT flood the eyes with the body color or navy.

night.why: Dark-bodied subject: the model floods the eyes navy without the note — burned ~26 attempts pre-notes (commit d96ae6f 'the dark-bodied spider needed "the eyes are the star"'; pipeline.md Stage 4 lever 4). Note text reconstructed from that prose; the original verbatim string was not preserved.

flags.notes: Paint every shell circle DEEP NEAR-BLACK — the ladybug's spots are black, never white or light.

night.why: pipeline.md Stage 4 lever 4: 'the ladybug's shell spots needed "paint every shell circle deep near-black"' (regen in d96ae6f after the per-eye relaxation shipped a dead-sclera ladybug).

flags.notes: The ladybug's shell spots are BLACK in canon — never fill a shell circle white. Whiten only the eye sclera and catchlights.

chalk.why: commit e99fd22: 'Ladybug redone at low temperature with a page note to keep its shell spots black (the model's one bad judgment call)'; pipeline.md Stage 1.5 names the white-shell-spot misfire. Orientation not recorded in the commit — entry placed on the cataloged tall page; apply to the wide too if it misfires the same way.

normalize.why: pipeline.md Stage 1 gate 6: 'caterpillar's faithful de-swirl took t 0.2 plus "CHANGE NOTHING ELSE ANYWHERE"' (commit 551ab52 — the first redraw produced hypno-swirl eyes, depth 5 -> 3 after).

night.review: Ships with a flat-pupil warning: the pen eye uses a SPIRAL catchlight and the fill model reliably refuses to paint the spiral interior dark (>=11 attempts, notes + low temp all failed — commit 8e02fd5). Do not burn attempts on the fill; the durable fix is de-swirling the pen (idea-run #5 validated a deterministic sharp/SVG surgery for this page), then regenerating the suite.

normalize.review: The outline audit PASSES this pen (ring depth 4, all thin strokes), so the normalizer skips it without --force — the spiral weld is invisible to every current gate (idea-run #5).

night.review: Same spiral-catchlight flat-pupil warning as caterpillar-wide (commit 8e02fd5), but harder: the eyes are true one-stroke spirals INCLUDING the outer ring, so the idea-#5 interior surgery does not apply — needs an outer-ring redraw or the forced normalizer before the fill can succeed.

flags.notes: Each eye must end as EXACTLY one thin pupil ring with one small catchlight circle inside it — redraw the small solid pupils as outlined rings; do not leave any solid ink in the eyes.

normalize.why: pipeline.md Stage 1 gate 6: 'bee-tall's pupils took an eye-specific note at t 0.7-0.8' (commit 6840bba: small pupils under ~4px strokes ducked the fixed erosion radius). Note text reconstructed.

flags.notes: The sky has THREE CLOUDS — keep all three exactly where they are.

normalize.why: pipeline.md Stage 1: 'the first bee-wide normalization silently deleted a cloud (worst-tile keep 0%), fixed with a --notes telling it the sky has three clouds'.
